seoTitle: Currency Creek vs Custon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Currency Creek and Custon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Currency Creek vs Custon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Currency Creek and Custon.
heading: Currency Creek vs Custon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Currency Creek, South Australia and Custon, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Currency Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Custon (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Currency Creek or Custon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Currency Creek has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Custon scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Currency Creek scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Custon scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Currency Creek or Custon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Currency Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
